meta.var_name: psk->identity, current->binder, binderLen, SetupPskKey, BuildTls13HandshakeHmac, TLSX_Find, TLSX_PreSharedKey_Parse, resumption
meta.desc: 查找服务器在处理 ClientHello 的 PSK 扩展后，如何选择匹配的 PSK identity、如何校验 binder，以及在 identity 未命中与 binder 校验失败时是否返回相同错误码或走相同分支。

 3703: /* The PreSharedKey extension information - entry in a linked list. */
 3704: typedef struct PreSharedKey {
 3705:     word16               identityLen;             /* Length of identity */
 3706:     byte*                identity;                /* PSK identity       */
 3707:     word32               ticketAge;               /* Age of the ticket  */
 3708:     byte                 cipherSuite0;            /* Cipher Suite       */
 3709:     byte                 cipherSuite;             /* Cipher Suite       */
 3710:     word32               binderLen;               /* Length of HMAC     */
 3711:     byte                 binder[WC_MAX_DIGEST_SIZE]; /* HMAC of handshake */
 3712:     byte                 hmac;                    /* HMAC algorithm     */
 3713: #ifdef HAVE_SESSION_TICKET
 3714:     InternalTicket*      it;                      /* ptr to ticket      */
 3715:     const WOLFSSL_SESSION* sess; /* ptr to session either from external cache or
 3716:                                   * into SessionCache. Work around so that we
 3717:                                   * don't call into the cache more than once */
 3718:     psk_sess_free_cb* sess_free_cb;               /* callback to free sess */
 3719:     psk_sess_free_cb_ctx sess_free_cb_ctx;        /* info for sess_free_cb */
 3720: #endif
 3721:     byte                 resumption:1;            /* Resumption PSK     */
 3722:     byte                 chosen:1;                /* Server's choice    */
 3723:     byte                 decryptRet:3;            /* Ticket decrypt return */
 3724:     struct PreSharedKey* next;                    /* List pointer       */
 3725: } PreSharedKey;
 3726:

 3739:                                   const byte* input, word16 length, void* heap);

   28: 
   29: #ifdef __cplusplus

   34:     #include <wolfssl/debug-untrace-error-codes.h>
   35: #endif

   37: enum wolfSSL_ErrorCodes {
   38:     WOLFSSL_FATAL_ERROR          =   -1,   /* must be -1 for backward compat. */
   39: 
   40:     /* negative counterparts to namesake positive constants in ssl.h */
   41:     WOLFSSL_ERROR_WANT_READ_E    =   -2,
   42:     WOLFSSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE_E   =   -3,
   43:     WOLFSSL_ERROR_WANT_X509_LOOKUP_E = -4,

   46:     WOLFSSL_ERROR_WANT_CONNECT_E =   -7,
   47:     WOLFSSL_ERROR_WANT_ACCEPT_E  =   -8,
   48: 
   49:     WOLFSSL_FIRST_E              = -301,   /* start of native TLS codes */
   50: 
   51:     INPUT_CASE_ERROR             = -301,   /* process input state error */
   52:     PREFIX_ERROR                 = -302,   /* bad index to key rounds  */
   53:     MEMORY_ERROR                 = -303,   /* out of memory            */
   54:     VERIFY_FINISHED_ERROR        = -304,   /* verify problem on finished */
   55:     VERIFY_MAC_ERROR             = -305,   /* verify mac problem       */
   56:     PARSE_ERROR                  = -306,   /* parse error on header    */
   57:     UNKNOWN_HANDSHAKE_TYPE       = -307,   /* weird handshake type     */
   58:     SOCKET_ERROR_E               = -308,   /* error state on socket    */
   59:     SOCKET_NODATA                = -309,   /* expected data, not there */
   60:     INCOMPLETE_DATA              = -310,   /* don't have enough data to
   61:                                               complete task            */
   62:     UNKNOWN_RECORD_TYPE          = -311,   /* unknown type in record hdr */
   63:     DECRYPT_ERROR                = -312,   /* error during decryption  */
   64:     FATAL_ERROR                  = -313,   /* recvd alert fatal error  */
   65:     ENCRYPT_ERROR                = -314,   /* error during encryption  */
   66:     FREAD_ERROR                  = -315,   /* fread problem            */
   67:     NO_PEER_KEY                  = -316,   /* need peer's key          */
   68:     NO_PRIVATE_KEY               = -317,   /* need the private key     */
   69:     RSA_PRIVATE_ERROR            = -318,   /* error during rsa priv op */

   73:     DOMAIN_NAME_MISMATCH         = -322,   /* peer subject name mismatch */

   75:     NOT_READY_ERROR              = -324,   /* handshake layer not ready */

   77:     VERSION_ERROR                = -326,   /* record layer version error */

   79:     BUFFER_ERROR                 = -328,   /* malformed buffer input   */

   81:     VERIFY_SIGN_ERROR            = -330,   /* verify sign error        */
   82:     CLIENT_ID_ERROR              = -331,   /* psk client identity error  */
   83:     SERVER_HINT_ERROR            = -332,   /* psk server hint error  */
   84:     PSK_KEY_ERROR                = -333,   /* psk key error  */
   85:     DUPE_ENTRY_E                 = -334,   /* duplicate entry error */
   86: 
   87:     GETTIME_ERROR                = -337,   /* gettimeofday failed ??? */

1: /* dtls.c
    2:  *
    3:  * Copyright (C) 2006-2026 wolfSSL Inc.
    4:  *
    5:  * This file is part of wolfSSL.
    6:  *
    7:  * wolfSSL is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify

   10:  * (at your option) any later version.
   11:  *
   12:  * wolfSSL is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
   13:  * but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
   14:  * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
   15:  * GNU General Public License for more details.
   16:  *
   17:  * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
   18:  * along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
   19:  * Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA 02110-1335, USA
   20:  */
   21: 
   22: #include <wolfssl/wolfcrypt/libwolfssl_sources.h>
   23: 
   24: /*
   25:  * WOLFSSL_DTLS_NO_HVR_ON_RESUME
   26:  * WOLFSSL_DTLS13_NO_HRR_ON_RESUME
   27:  *     If defined, a DTLS server will not do a cookie exchange on successful
   28:  *     client resumption: the resumption will be faster (one RTT less) and
   29:  *     will consume less bandwidth (one ClientHello and one
   30:  *     HelloVerifyRequest/HelloRetryRequest less). On the other hand, if a valid
   31:  *     SessionID/ticket/psk is collected, forged clientHello messages will
   32:  *     consume resources on the server. For DTLS 1.3, using this option also
   33:  *     allows for the server to process Early Data/0-RTT Data. Without this, the
   34:  *     Early Data would be dropped since the server doesn't enter stateful
   35:  *     processing until receiving a verified ClientHello with the cookie.
   36:  *
   37:  *     To allow DTLS 1.3 resumption without the cookie exchange:
   38:  *     - Compile wolfSSL with WOLFSSL_DTLS13_NO_HRR_ON_RESUME defined
   39:  *     - Call wolfSSL_dtls13_no_hrr_on_resume(ssl, 1) on the WOLFSSL object to
   40:  *       disable the cookie exchange on resumption
   41:  *     - Continue like with a normal connection
   42:  * WOLFSSL_DTLS_CH_FRAG
   43:  *     Allow a server to process a fragmented second/verified (one containing a
   44:  *     valid cookie response) ClientHello message. The first/unverified (one
   45:  *     without a cookie extension) ClientHello MUST be unfragmented so that the
   46:  *     DTLS server can process it statelessly. This is only implemented for
   47:  *     DTLS 1.3. The user MUST call wolfSSL_dtls13_allow_ch_frag() on the server
   48:  *     to explicitly enable this during runtime.
   49:  */
   50: 
   51: #ifndef WOLFCRYPT_ONLY
   52: 
   53: #include <wolfssl/error-ssl.h>
   54: #include <wolfssl/internal.h>
   55: #include <wolfssl/ssl.h>
   56: #ifdef NO_INLINE
   57:     #include <wolfssl/wolfcrypt/misc.h>
   58: #else
   59:     #define WOLFSSL_MISC_INCLUDED
   60:     #include <wolfcrypt/src/misc.c>
   61: #endif
   62: 
   63: #define ERROR_OUT(err, eLabel) { ret = (err); goto eLabel; }
   64: 
   65: #ifdef WOLFSSL_DTLS
   66: 
   67: void DtlsResetState(WOLFSSL* ssl)
   68: {
   69:     /* Reset the state so that we can statelessly await the
   70:      * ClientHello that contains the cookie. Don't gate on IsAtLeastTLSv1_3
   71:      * to handle the edge case when the peer wants a lower version. */
   72: 
   73:     /* Reset DTLS window */
   74: #ifdef WOLFSSL_DTLS13
   75:     w64Zero(&ssl->dtls13Epochs[0].nextSeqNumber);
   76:     w64Zero(&ssl->dtls13Epochs[0].nextPeerSeqNumber);
   77:     XMEMSET(ssl->dtls13Epochs[0].window, 0,
   78:         sizeof(ssl->dtls13Epochs[0].window));
   79:     Dtls13FreeFsmResources(ssl);
   80: #endif
   81:     ssl->keys.dtls_expected_peer_handshake_number = 0;
   82:     ssl->keys.dtls_handshake_number = 0;
   83:     ssl->keys.dtls_sequence_number_hi = 0;
   84:     ssl->keys.dtls_sequence_number_lo = 0;
   85: 
   86:     /* Reset states */
   87:     ssl->options.serverState = NULL_STATE;
   88:     ssl->options.clientState = NULL_STATE;
   89:     ssl->options.connectState = CONNECT_BEGIN;
   90:     ssl->options.acceptState = ACCEPT_BEGIN;
